text: Violence Surges in Nigeria as Kidnappings & Bandit Raids Wreak Havoc Nigeria faces surge in violence with 150 abducted in Zamfara, 31 rescued by the army in Kaduna, and 17 killed in Benue, highlighting worsening insecurity & displacement across multiple regions as terror groups & criminal gangs target rural population. 🛢 The International Energy Agency outlines a two-track response to the Middle East oil shock: boost supply where possible, but — crucially — cut demand to protect consumers from price spikes . On the supply side , the main action is the record release of 400 million barrels from emergency reserves , aimed at stabilizing markets. However, the IEA stresses this alone is not enough given the scale of disruption. The bulk of the recommendations focus on immediate demand-side measures , which can quickly reduce oil consumption and ease prices ➡️ Work from home to cut commuting fuel use ➡️ Reduce driving speeds to improve fuel efficiency ➡️ Carpool more / use public transport ➡️ Limit air travel where possible ➡️ Encourage flexible work schedules to reduce peak transport demand ➡️ Promote energy-saving behaviors in households and businesses Governments are also advised to provide targeted financial support for vulnerable households, and implement short-term policies that curb fuel demand without harming economic activity 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 According to Reuters, a rescue mission in Iran almost failed after two special operations aircraft (MC-130 COMMANDOS II) used for infiltration experienced mechanical failures (presumably, the landing gear was damaged upon landing at a makeshift field airstrip) and were unable to take off. 🔹 This put part of the rescue forces at risk of being stranded on Iranian territory, which one American senior official described as the mission's truly "shocking" moment. Due to time constraints and increased risk of exposure, US command decided to send additional aircraft to gradually evacuate the unit. 🔹 Reports indicate the rescue team had to wait several tense hours until the backup plan was successfully executed. 🔹 After the evacuation was completed, American forces destroyed the two damaged MC-130 COMMANDO II aircraft on site along with four small MH-6 LITTLE BIRD helicopters on board to prevent sensitive equipment from falling into enemy hands. ▪️ Under the cover of darkness, about 100 US Special Operations soldiers infiltrated deep into Iran and moved through the difficult mountainous terrain south of Isfahan. ▪️ They crossed a ridge at 7,000 feet (approximately 2,100 meters) elevation to reach the stranded pilot and establish successful contact. ▪️ The F-15E STRIKE EAGLE crew member, an armament officer, had a broken leg and after ejecting, was hiding in a crevice on a mountain peak using survival and evasion techniques. ▪️ Before the rescue forces took direct action, he identified himself to avoid American troops falling into an Iranian trap. ⛑️ On the third day, the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) located the pilot in Isfahan province. American attack aircraft were striking Iranian convoys to prevent them from approaching the pilot's location. tweet

For context, this well-known Qari has never once condemned the U.S-Israeli aggression on Iran that has claimed civilian lives, including…" 🇰🇼 Quran.com has removed Mishary Rashid Alafasy as its default Quran reciter after he posted controversial comments on X supporting Trump's attacks on Iran . He had long been the default reciter, with his voice automatically playing for millions of users. Following widespread backlash from Muslims outraged by his pro-Trump, anti-Iran remarks amid ongoing U.S.-Israeli aggression, the popular website quietly demoted him from the default position in early April 2026. His recitations are still available for manual selection, but no longer play automatically. Quran.com has not issued any official statement.

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 After reviewing flight data of a Northrop Grumman MQ-4C Triton of US Navy, it was found the drone is operating over the Persian Gulf near Iran’s coastline. — This drone took off from the Sigonella Air Base in Sicily. Currently, this drone is flying in a figure-eight pattern near the city of Bushehr, Iran, where the country's only nuclear power plant is located nearby. — This drone is moving at an altitude of 14,000 meters and at an approximate speed of 400 kilometers per hour.

Iran says it doesn’t expect a total blackout even if the U.S. attacks its power plants. An official told Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting that “highly diversified energy production sources,” along with the grid’s “geographic dispersion” and “interconnectedness,” would allow the country to keep supplying at least partial power.

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Iran’s FM spokesperson, Esmail Baghaei: The US 15-point plan is absolutely unacceptable / We conveyed our positions through intermediaries — When the US 15-point plan was proposed, we stated that such proposals are both extremely greedy and unreasonable and illogical, and absolutely unacceptable to us. — Based on our interests and considerations, we have documented the set of demands we had and have; we are not ashamed to loudly express our legitimate and logical demands and requests. — That the Islamic Republic of Iran quickly and courageously presents its views in response to a plan should by no means be interpreted as yielding to the enemy. — We have prepared our responses and will explicitly inform when it is necessary to announce them; it is not strange that intermediaries convey the viewpoints of both sides. These positions were initially raised and conveyed through intermediaries, and naturally, this message transfer continues.

🇮🇷 🇵🇰 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Reuters is reporting that Pakistan's army chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, has been in contact "all night long" with U.S. Vice President JD Vance, special envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi for a settlement deal. 🔸 Under the proposal, a ceasefire would take effect immediately, reopening the Strait of Hormuz, with 15–20 days to finalise a broader settlement. The deal, tentatively dubbed the "Islamabad Accord," would include a regional framework for the strait, with final in-person talks in Islamabad. 🔸 There was no immediate response from U.S. and ‌Iranian officials. ⁠Pakistan's foreign office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi declined comment. Iranian officials have previously told Reuters that Tehran was seeking a permanent ceasefire with guarantees they will not be attacked again by the U.S. and Israel. 🔸 Iran has received messages from mediators including Pakistan, Turkey and Egypt according to the outlet. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

A third Turkish-owned tanker, Ocean Thunder, cleared the Strait of Hormuz overnight. It was carrying Iraqi crude to Malaysia. Türkiye now has 3 ships safely out of the Gulf area since the conflict began in late Feb 2026. 12 ships remain stuck.

Trump warned he could destroy Iran’s power plants if it doesn’t reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday. However, mediators say he still wants a cease-fire and may compromise. Iran remains skeptical, suspecting the U.S. could use any pause to prepare further attacks. Source: WSJ

🇮🇱 ⚡️ 🇮🇷 Israel's $15 billion damage in the war with Iran 🔹 The cost of the ongoing war with Iran and Hezbollah in Lebanon has risen to approximately $15 billion, and this figure is expected to increase in the future as the conflicts continue and their economic consequences expand. 🔹 The Israeli Ministry of Defense has requested approximately $12.4 billion to cover military expenses. 🔹 Around 26,000 compensation claims have been registered for damages caused by Iranian missile bombardments, with their value estimated between $320 million and $450 million.

France’s central bank sold 129 tonnes of French gold stored in New York, bought equivalent gold in Europe that meets current standards, and recorded a €12.8bn gain due to higher gold prices. All French gold reserves are now held in Paris.
